message:
feat(ars): add expected loss dynamic timeout calculator

Implements ft-tkpgy.3.1 — log-normal duration model with expected loss
minimization (golden section search) for optimal reflex timeouts. Includes
standard normal CDF/quantile, TimeoutTracker with ring buffer, and
configurable cost asymmetry (hang vs premature kill). 44 inline + 24 proptests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-2p9cb.2.2.1","title":"[AARSP][Subtask][Spec] ft-2p9cb.2.2 :: Contracts, invariants, benchmark and test matrix","description":"## Objective\nTurn parent bead into a deterministic execution contract with measurable outcomes.\n\n## Deliverables\n1. Boundary map for code paths, interfaces, and failure domains touched by the parent task.\n2. Explicit invariants and impossible-state definitions with reason-code taxonomy.\n3. Benchmark contract: workload classes, p50/p95/p99 targets, pass/fail criteria, and overhead budgets.\n4. Verification matrix: unit, property, integration, e2e, chaos, and soak scenarios including nominal/degraded/recovery cases.\n5. Structured logging contract (required fields): timestamp, subsystem, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n1. Spec is precise enough that two engineers implement the same behavior.\n2. Every success/failure mode has explicit test ownership.\n3. Benchmark and test matrix include edge-case and adversarial conditions.\n4. User-facing impact is mapped to measurable metrics and guardrails.\n\n## User Impact\nReduces ambiguity, prevents regressions, and accelerates reliable delivery.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:26.356377Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T21:11:55.780724Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T21:11:55.780710Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","determinism","e2e-tests","extreme-optimization","planning","quality-gate","subtask","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.2.1","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:26.356377Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-2p9cb.2.2.2","title":"[AARSP][Subtask][Impl] ft-2p9cb.2.2 :: Deterministic implementation and guardrails","description":"## Objective\nImplement the parent capability with deterministic semantics, bounded resource behavior, and explicit fallback policy.\n\n## Deliverables\n1. Core implementation for planned fast/slow paths with explicit guardrails.\n2. Failure-handling and degradation ladder with stable reason codes.\n3. Performance-sensitive path optimizations validated against the benchmark contract.\n4. Operator-visible controls and diagnostics required by parent scope.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n1. Behavior matches the subtask spec contract and parent acceptance criteria.\n2. No feature loss and no silent behavior drift.\n3. Deterministic behavior under fixed input/seed/config for replay scenarios.\n4. Structured logs are emitted at all critical decisions and failure transitions.\n\n## User Impact\nDelivers real responsiveness/reliability gains without sacrificing predictability.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:26.435553Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T21:18:48.114013Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T21:18:48.113994Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","determinism","e2e-tests","extreme-optimization","implementation","quality-gate","subtask","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:26.435553Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:26.584852Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-2p9cb.2.2.3","title":"[AARSP][Subtask][Verify] ft-2p9cb.2.2 :: Unit/property/integration/e2e + evidence gates","description":"## Objective\nProve correctness, performance, and resilience for the parent task with reproducible evidence.\n\n## Deliverables\n1. Comprehensive unit tests for happy path, edge cases, and failure conditions.\n2. Property/concurrency tests where applicable for invariants and race-sensitive behavior.\n3. Integration + e2e scripts with detailed scenario logging (nominal, stress, failure, recovery, soak).\n4. Artifact bundle: benchmark outputs, golden traces/fixtures, log manifests, and mismatch diagnostics.\n5. CI/promotion gate updates that fail on missing artifacts, budget regressions, or proof violations.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n1. All required test layers exist and pass reproducibly.\n2. Logs provide high-fidelity triage context and decision lineage.\n3. Evidence artifacts are machine-verifiable and retained for review.\n4. Parent task can be closed only after evidence gate criteria are met.\n\n## User Impact\nEnsures optimizations are safe, durable, and trust-preserving in production.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:26.508215Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T21:21:22.000181Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T21:21:22.000165Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","determinism","e2e-tests","extreme-optimization","quality-gate","subtask","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.2.3","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:26.508215Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.2.3","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:26.676808Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-2p9cb.2.3","title":"[AARSP][B3] Priority inheritance and lock-order correctness","description":"## Objective
Add priority inheritance and lock-order protocol to eliminate inversion on critical interactions.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Priority inversion scenarios are detected and resolved deterministically.
2. Lock-order violations are prevented with explicit diagnostics.
3. Concurrency tests and targeted chaos scenarios validate robustness.
4. Logs include inheritance events and inversion-prevention outcomes.

## User Impact
High-priority interactions do not stall behind low-priority work.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:05.777889Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:27.508266Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","concurrency","determinism","e2e-tests","extreme-optimization","performance","quality-gate","reactivity","reliability","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.3","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.3","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1196,"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Execution split added for implementation rigor and testability:\n1. ft-2p9cb.2.3.1 (spec/contracts/benchmark/test matrix)\n2. ft-2p9cb.2.3.2 (deterministic implementation + guardrails)\n3. ft-2p9cb.2.3.3 (unit/property/integration/e2e + evidence/CI gates)\n\nDependency chain: ft-2p9cb.2.3.2 -> ft-2p9cb.2.3.1 and ft-2p9cb.2.3.3 -> ft-2p9cb.2.3.2.\nParent-child linkage keeps the subtask relationship explicit while preserving DAG integrity.","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:27Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-2p9cb.2.4","title":"[AARSP][B4] Starvation prevention, fairness metrics, and replay-proof behavior","description":"## Objective
Implement fairness and starvation-prevention policy with deterministic ejection/deferral rules.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. No lane starves under sustained mixed load.
2. Fairness metrics are exposed and tested.
3. Deterministic replay confirms identical scheduling outcomes.
4. E2E evidence includes long-haul fairness/latency curves.

## User Impact
Consistent responsiveness and throughput over long-running sessions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:05.913011Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:28.051440Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","determinism","e2e-tests","extreme-optimization","fairness","performance","quality-gate","reactivity","reliability","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.4","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.4","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1197,"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.4","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Execution split added for implementation rigor and testability:\n1. ft-2p9cb.2.4.1 (spec/contracts/benchmark/test matrix)\n2. ft-2p9cb.2.4.2 (deterministic implementation + guardrails)\n3. ft-2p9cb.2.4.3 (unit/property/integration/e2e + evidence/CI gates)\n\nDependency chain: ft-2p9cb.2.4.2 -> ft-2p9cb.2.4.1 and ft-2p9cb.2.4.3 -> ft-2p9cb.2.4.2.\nParent-child linkage keeps the subtask relationship explicit while preserving DAG integrity.","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:28Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-2p9cb.3","title":"[AARSP][Track C] Zero-Copy Data Plane and Memory Topology","description":"## Track Goal
Make the data plane aggressively efficient with zero-copy paths, tiered memory, and transport adaptation.

## Why This Matters
Excess copying, fragmentation, and naive compression policies dominate tail latency and instability at scale.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Critical data paths are zero-copy or bounded-copy with evidence.
2. Memory tiers are explicit, measurable, and stable under pressure.
3. Transport policies adapt to local vs remote conditions deterministically.

## User Impact
Lower latency, smoother behavior at high pane counts, and fewer memory-induced stalls.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:06.049585Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:06.203299Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","determinism","e2e-tests","extreme-optimization","io","memory","performance","quality-gate","reactivity","reliability","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.3","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.3","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":448,"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Future-self: optimize bytes moved per user-visible operation, not just CPU cycles.","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:06Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-tkpgy.2.3","title":"[ARS][T2.3] Lightweight AST Symbolic Execution for Traversal/Destruction Safety","description":"## Background & Justification
A regex blocklist is not enough. To formally prove a reflex is safe, we parse its Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) using a fast shell lexer and symbolically execute it against a mock filesystem tree.

## Considerations
We must mathematically prove the command does not mutate paths outside the snapshotted CWD (e.g., rejecting `rm -rf ../*`) and does not invoke catastrophic binaries (`mkfs`, `dd`).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The AST lexer correctly parses standard `sh`/`bash` syntax.
2. Symbolic execution engine emits a formal safety proof failure if it detects directory traversal or recursive deletion targeting root.
3. Safely passes benign commands (e.g., `cargo build`, `npm install`).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.444971Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T21:17:07.683454Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T21:17:07.683405Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.2.3","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.444971Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.2.3","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.444971Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-tkpgy.2.4","title":"[ARS][T2.4] Parameter Generalization and PAC-Bayesian Bounds Generation","description":"## Background & Justification
A hardcoded fix for `/app/src/main.rs` is useless if the error happens in `/app/src/lib.rs`. We must generalize parameters using regex capture groups.
Furthermore, we must generate strict Regex safety bounds for the variables (PAC-Bayesian bounded) to prevent injection vulnerabilities.

## Considerations
Compare the error text against the extracted command. If substrings match (e.g., filenames, line numbers), convert them to Jinja templates `{{cap.file}}`. Automatically attach a safety constraint like `^[a-zA-Z0-9_./-]+$`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Correctly identifies and templates dynamic variables shared between the MinHash cluster sample and the MDL command.
2. Auto-generates regex bounds that strictly reject path traversals, wildcards, or subshell injections (`$(...)`, `` `...` ``).
3. Provides trace logs documenting the exact generalization map and bounded constraints.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.496026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T21:29:48.939023Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T21:29:48.939002Z","close_reason":"Implemented ars_generalize.rs with 70 inline tests + 28 proptests. Parameter detection (file paths, line numbers, identifiers, numerics), Jinja template generation, safety regex validation, PAC-Bayesian risk bounds.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.2.4","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.496026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.2.4","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.496026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-tkpgy.3","title":"[ARS] Compilation: FST Generation & Evidence Ledgers","description":"## Objective
Compile the safe, minimal template into an ultra-fast execution artifact and an immutable Evidence Ledger.

## Subtasks
- **Finite State Transducer (FST) Compilation:** Compile the MinHash signatures and trigger rules of all active reflexes into a Finite State Transducer. This guarantees that checking an incoming error against the entire ARS database takes $O(Length)$ time, completely independent of the number of reflexes stored.
- **Dynamic YAML Generation:** Construct the `WorkflowDescriptor` injecting `SnapshotTerminal` and `WaitFor` verification steps.
- **Dynamic Timeout via Expected Loss:** Calculate the dynamic timeout using an expected loss minimization function based on the tracked duration: $rg\min_t L(t) = 	ext{Cost(Hang)} \cdot P(T > t) + 	ext{Cost(PrematureKill)} \cdot P(T \le t)$.
- **Evidence Ledger Generation:** Append a cryptographic Evidence Ledger to the compiled reflex, detailing the exact Bayes Factors, MDL scores, and Symbolic Execution proofs that justified its synthesis.

## Considerations & Future-Proofing
FST compilation is an extreme software optimization that allows the immune system to scale to millions of known errors without ever slowing down the terminal processing loop.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. **Performance:** Benchmarks (`hyperfine` / `cargo bench`) prove the FST lookup evaluates against 100,000 mock reflex signatures in < 100µs.
2. **Compilation Integrity:** YAML serialization unit tests verify structural perfection, correct dynamic timeout calculations via the loss function, and snapshot/verification injections.
3. **Evidence Ledger:** The generated reflex contains a verifiable Evidence Ledger documenting the formal proofs of safety and minimal extraction.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:44:44.765616Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:10:22.725231Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.3","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.3","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-tkpgy.3.1","title":"[ARS][T3.1] Expected Loss Dynamic Timeout Calculator","description":"## Background & Justification
Reflexes must not hang. We use the wall-clock execution duration captured in T1.4 to calculate a dynamic timeout.
Using an expected loss minimization function: $rg\min_t L(t) = 	ext{Cost(Hang)} \cdot P(T > t) + 	ext{Cost(PrematureKill)} \cdot P(T \le t)$.

## Considerations
Allows the system to derive an optimal timeout (e.g., 2.5x the observed duration, clamped between 500ms and 60s) formally rather than using magic numbers.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Unit tests calculate the optimal timeout given a distribution of observed durations and specific cost weights.

 {"id":"ft-tkpgy.3.2","title":"[ARS][T3.2] Evidence Ledger Generation & Cryptographic Structuring","description":"## Background & Justification
Every learned reflex must carry a verifiable proof of *why* it was synthesized.
The Evidence Ledger bundles the BOCPD Bayes Factors, the MDL extraction score, the Symbolic Execution AST safety proof, and the parameter bounds into a cryptographic struct.

## Considerations
This ledger will be serialized alongside the YAML. It provides the data payload for the "Galaxy Brain" UX (`ft why`).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Defines the `EvidenceLedger` struct with serialization (JSON/YAML).
2. Successfully aggregates data from T1, T2, and T3 pipelines into a unified, immutable record.
3. Includes a SHA256 hash of the inputs to prove the ledger hasn't been tampered with.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.605317Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:10:27.239398Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.3.2","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.605317Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.3.2","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.605317Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-tkpgy.3.3","title":"[ARS][T3.3] Dynamic YAML Compilation & Injection (Snapshot/WaitFor)","description":"## Background & Justification
Compile the templated command, the timeout, and the constraints into a `WorkflowDescriptor`.

## Considerations
Must auto-inject a `DescriptorStep::SnapshotTerminal` before the action, and a `DescriptorStep::WaitFor` (or check exit code) after.
Also handles the **Operator Override Gate**: abort compilation if an operator has locked a manual YAML for this cluster.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Emits schema-valid `WorkflowDescriptor` YAML containing the injected pre/post steps.
2. Honors the operator override lock, cleanly aborting synthesis and logging the bypass.
3. Variables mapped via Jinja match the generalized parameters from T2.4.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.656975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:10:26.890393Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.3.3","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.656975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.3.3","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.656975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-tkpgy.3.4","title":"[ARS][T3.4] O(1) Finite State Transducer (FST) Compiler for Reflex Signatures","description":"## Background & Justification
As the ARS database grows to millions of reflexes across the federation, linear or hash-map lookups might stall.
Compiling the MinHash signatures and trigger contexts into a Finite State Transducer (FST) guarantees $O(K)$ lookup time where $K$ is the length of the query, utterly independent of the total number of stored reflexes.

## Considerations
Whenever a new reflex is promoted to Active, the background compiler thread rebuilds the FST and hot-swaps it atomically via an `ArcSwap`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. `cargo bench` proves lookup time is bounded by $O(K)$ and unaffected by scaling the reflex cache from 10 to 1,000,000 entries.
2. Background thread successfully rebuilds and atomically swaps the FST without locking readers.
3. Correctly maps MinHash queries to their respective `WorkflowDescriptor` IDs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.706260Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:10:26.527319Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.3.4","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.706260Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.3.4","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.706260Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
